name: fix adivina_stat to auto-query real messages-sent-yesterday stat
botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes:
No new repository, no new Prisma model, no new imports needed — everything the fix requires already exists and is already wired into BotUpdateService.

Where it goes: replace the existing `case "adivina_stat":` block verbatim (currently apps/bot/src/bot-update.service.ts lines ~16297-16309, inside `private async handleUtilityPlusCommand(context: FoundationContext, update: TelegramUpdateEnvelope)`, which already destructures `const args = update.command?.args ?? []`). Just swap the old block for the new one at that same switch-case location; no new case needs to be added to the switch since the case already exists.

Why this needs zero new plumbing:
1. The schema already tracks exactly this ("messages sent yesterday" per chat) via `model ActivityDaily { tenantId, chatId, day, messages }` (packages/data/prisma/schema.prisma:1319-1331), upserted once per message.
2. The repository for it already exists and is the established pattern (interface + PrismaXRepository + InMemoryXRepository): `packages/data/src/analytics-repository.ts`, exported as `AnalyticsRepository` from `@superbot/data`. Its `getRecentDays(chatId, limit): Promise<ActivityWindowRow[]>` (row = `{ day: string; messages: number }`) is exactly the real-value source needed — no new repository file required.
3. `AnalyticsRepository` is already constructor-injected into `BotUpdateService` as `private readonly analyticsRepository` (bot-update.service.ts:812) and already used the same way by the neighboring `handleStatsCommand` (bot-update.service.ts:7800-7853), which computes "today" via `dayKeyFromMs(now)` off the same `getRecentDays` result — so this fix reuses an established call pattern in the same file, not a new one.
4. `dayKeyFromMs` (UTC day-bucket helper) is already imported at bot-update.service.ts:170 from `@superbot/module-community` (modules/community/src/analytics.ts:66-67); `scoreStatGuess` is already imported at bot-update.service.ts:365 from `@superbot/module-games` (modules/games/src/group-stat-trivia.ts:34-48). Both imports are untouched — no edit to the import block is needed.

Behavior change: the command signature goes from `/adivina_stat <adivinado> <real>` (both hand-typed — the actual bug) to `/adivina_stat <adivinado>` only. `actual` is now derived server-side as the group's real message count for "yesterday" (UTC day bucket, `now - 86_400_000`), looked up from `getRecentDays(chatId, 3)` (a small window of 3 is enough to always include yesterday's row even if today already has one; if the chat had zero messages that day, no row exists and `actual` correctly falls back to 0 via `?? 0` rather than throwing). Everything downstream (`scoreStatGuess`, the ✅/❌ reply, point calculation) is unchanged from the original — only the source of `actual` changes from `Number.parseFloat(args[1])` to a live DB-backed computation, and the reply text is extended to state the real number so players can verify the group's admin isn't fabricating it.

Docs note (optional, not required by the task's file restrictions): `docs/Modryva-Vault/05 Modules/Games/Trivia de Estadísticas de Grupo.md` explicitly flags this as an open question ("¿Se conecta a alguna métrica real del grupo... ?") and states the command line reference `bot-update.service.ts:15839-15851` (older line numbers than current ~16297-16309, file has grown since that note was written) — worth updating that vault note's "Estado / cableado" and "Preguntas abiertas" sections after this lands, but it's documentation, not code, and outside the files this task touches.
risks: Low risk, no schema/migration involved since it reuses an already-live table and repository. The only externally-visible change is the command's arg count (breaking for any admin used to typing two numbers) — worth a heads-up in the group when deployed to the test bot (@Modryvatestbot) so admins know `/adivina_stat <adivinado>` no longer takes a second "real" argument (a stray second arg is simply ignored since only args[0] is read, so it fails soft rather than erroring). Also note: `getRecentDays` returns rows sorted `day: "desc"` with `take: limit`, so `limit: 3` is a hardcoded safety margin, not a guarantee — if the bot's clock/timezone ever drifts from UTC-day bucketing assumptions this could very rarely miss "yesterday" and silently report 0; this mirrors the exact same limit/lookup pattern already trusted in the neighboring `handleStatsCommand`, so it's consistent with existing risk tolerance rather than a new one.
